{'text': '愛知県江南市宮田町四ツ谷285', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:川島神社 (江南市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11478285}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 |名称 = 川島神社 |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|川島神社鳥居]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}宮田町四ツ谷285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社
{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|牛頭天王|Q11570247}} |例祭 = 3月26日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=川島神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''川島神社'''（かわしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「川嶋神社」の論社の一つであるが、異説も多い。 === 歴史 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''牛頭天王'''と称しており、度重なる洪水で廃れたという。所在地はかつて「天神森」と呼ばれていた。江戸中期に『延喜式神名帳』の葉栗郡「川嶋神社」に比定する説が浮上し、再建された。{{jalink|1898年|Q7848}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}31年）には正式に「川島神社」と改称した。 ただし式内社の「川嶋神社」は論社は複数あり学説としては確定していない。これは付近に{{jalink|木曽川|Q779607}}があり、度重なる洪水で社殿の流失があったため正確には不明であるためとされる。論社としては以下のようなものがある。 * 川島神社：愛知県江南市宮田町 * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市川島笠田町)|Q124668655|白鬚神社}}：{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|川島笠田町|Q131938355}} * {{jalink|榎神社 (各務原市)|Q134893091|榎神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|川島松倉町|Q131870131}} * 川島天神社（{{jalink|若栗神社|Q11616546}}境内社）：愛知県{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}島村 * {{jalink|上ノ島神明神社|Q11358370}}：岐阜県各務原市川島松倉町 * {{jalink|社宮神社 (各務原市)||社宮神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|成清町|Q131983825}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市松本町)|Q134987078|白鬚神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|松本町 (各務原市)|Q131870034|松本町}} * {{jalink|八剣神社 (岐南町)|Q11390592|八剣神社}}：岐阜県{{jalink|羽島郡|Q615068}}{{jalink|岐南町|Q1204285}}みやまち 『{{jalink|尾張国風土記|Q135598023}}』逸文によれば、式内社の「川嶋神社」は葉栗郡河沼郷川嶋村に所在し、{{jalink|聖武天皇|Q349263}}の時代に{{jalink|白鹿|Q72616124}}が時々現れたので勅命で建てられた神社という。 === 祭神 === * 主神 ** {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} * 合祀 ** {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐男尊}} ** {{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主神}} ** {{jalink|徳川義直|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|徳川光友|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|川島神社本殿]] === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「本郷口」バス停下車、徒歩15分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}} {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷」行き ** 名古屋鉄道{{jalink|名鉄犬山線|Q6810411|犬山線}} {{jalink|江南駅 (愛知県)|Q201473|江南駅}}より「川島」行き === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {'text': '愛知県江南市宮田町四ツ谷285', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:川島神社 (江南市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11478285}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 |名称 = 川島神社 |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|川島神社鳥居]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}宮田町四ツ谷285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社
{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|牛頭天王|Q11570247}} |例祭 = 3月26日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=川島神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''川島神社'''（かわしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「川嶋神社」の論社の一つであるが、異説も多い。 === 歴史 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''牛頭天王'''と称しており、度重なる洪水で廃れたという。所在地はかつて「天神森」と呼ばれていた。江戸中期に『延喜式神名帳』の葉栗郡「川嶋神社」に比定する説が浮上し、再建された。{{jalink|1898年|Q7848}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}31年）には正式に「川島神社」と改称した。 ただし式内社の「川嶋神社」は論社は複数あり学説としては確定していない。これは付近に{{jalink|木曽川|Q779607}}があり、度重なる洪水で社殿の流失があったため正確には不明であるためとされる。論社としては以下のようなものがある。 * 川島神社：愛知県江南市宮田町 * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市川島笠田町)|Q124668655|白鬚神社}}：{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|川島笠田町|Q131938355}} * {{jalink|榎神社 (各務原市)|Q134893091|榎神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|川島松倉町|Q131870131}} * 川島天神社（{{jalink|若栗神社|Q11616546}}境内社）：愛知県{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}島村 * {{jalink|上ノ島神明神社|Q11358370}}：岐阜県各務原市川島松倉町 * {{jalink|社宮神社 (各務原市)||社宮神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|成清町|Q131983825}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市松本町)|Q134987078|白鬚神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|松本町 (各務原市)|Q131870034|松本町}} * {{jalink|八剣神社 (岐南町)|Q11390592|八剣神社}}：岐阜県{{jalink|羽島郡|Q615068}}{{jalink|岐南町|Q1204285}}みやまち 『{{jalink|尾張国風土記|Q135598023}}』逸文によれば、式内社の「川嶋神社」は葉栗郡河沼郷川嶋村に所在し、{{jalink|聖武天皇|Q349263}}の時代に{{jalink|白鹿|Q72616124}}が時々現れたので勅命で建てられた神社という。 === 祭神 === * 主神 ** {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} * 合祀 ** {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐男尊}} ** {{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主神}} ** {{jalink|徳川義直|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|徳川光友|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|川島神社本殿]] === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「本郷口」バス停下車、徒歩15分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}} {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷」行き ** 名古屋鉄道{{jalink|名鉄犬山線|Q6810411|犬山線}} {{jalink|江南駅 (愛知県)|Q201473|江南駅}}より「川島」行き === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {'text': '愛知県江南市宮田町四ツ谷285', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:川島神社 (江南市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11478285}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 |名称 = 川島神社 |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|川島神社鳥居]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}宮田町四ツ谷285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社
{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|牛頭天王|Q11570247}} |例祭 = 3月26日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=川島神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''川島神社'''（かわしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「川嶋神社」の論社の一つであるが、異説も多い。 === 歴史 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''牛頭天王'''と称しており、度重なる洪水で廃れたという。所在地はかつて「天神森」と呼ばれていた。江戸中期に『延喜式神名帳』の葉栗郡「川嶋神社」に比定する説が浮上し、再建された。{{jalink|1898年|Q7848}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}31年）には正式に「川島神社」と改称した。 ただし式内社の「川嶋神社」は論社は複数あり学説としては確定していない。これは付近に{{jalink|木曽川|Q779607}}があり、度重なる洪水で社殿の流失があったため正確には不明であるためとされる。論社としては以下のようなものがある。 * 川島神社：愛知県江南市宮田町 * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市川島笠田町)|Q124668655|白鬚神社}}：{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|川島笠田町|Q131938355}} * {{jalink|榎神社 (各務原市)|Q134893091|榎神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|川島松倉町|Q131870131}} * 川島天神社（{{jalink|若栗神社|Q11616546}}境内社）：愛知県{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}島村 * {{jalink|上ノ島神明神社|Q11358370}}：岐阜県各務原市川島松倉町 * {{jalink|社宮神社 (各務原市)||社宮神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|成清町|Q131983825}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市松本町)|Q134987078|白鬚神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|松本町 (各務原市)|Q131870034|松本町}} * {{jalink|八剣神社 (岐南町)|Q11390592|八剣神社}}：岐阜県{{jalink|羽島郡|Q615068}}{{jalink|岐南町|Q1204285}}みやまち 『{{jalink|尾張国風土記|Q135598023}}』逸文によれば、式内社の「川嶋神社」は葉栗郡河沼郷川嶋村に所在し、{{jalink|聖武天皇|Q349263}}の時代に{{jalink|白鹿|Q72616124}}が時々現れたので勅命で建てられた神社という。 === 祭神 === * 主神 ** {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} * 合祀 ** {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐男尊}} ** {{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主神}} ** {{jalink|徳川義直|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|徳川光友|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|川島神社本殿]] === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「本郷口」バス停下車、徒歩15分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}} {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷」行き ** 名古屋鉄道{{jalink|名鉄犬山線|Q6810411|犬山線}} {{jalink|江南駅 (愛知県)|Q201473|江南駅}}より「川島」行き === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:江南市の歴史]] [[Category:江南市の建築物]] {'text': '愛知県江南市宮田町四ツ谷285', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:川島神社 (江南市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11478285}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 |名称 = 川島神社 |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|川島神社鳥居]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}宮田町四ツ谷285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社
{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|牛頭天王|Q11570247}} |例祭 = 3月26日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=川島神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''川島神社'''（かわしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「川嶋神社」の論社の一つであるが、異説も多い。 === 歴史 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''牛頭天王'''と称しており、度重なる洪水で廃れたという。所在地はかつて「天神森」と呼ばれていた。江戸中期に『延喜式神名帳』の葉栗郡「川嶋神社」に比定する説が浮上し、再建された。{{jalink|1898年|Q7848}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}31年）には正式に「川島神社」と改称した。 ただし式内社の「川嶋神社」は論社は複数あり学説としては確定していない。これは付近に{{jalink|木曽川|Q779607}}があり、度重なる洪水で社殿の流失があったため正確には不明であるためとされる。論社としては以下のようなものがある。 * 川島神社：愛知県江南市宮田町 * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市川島笠田町)|Q124668655|白鬚神社}}：{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|川島笠田町|Q131938355}} * {{jalink|榎神社 (各務原市)|Q134893091|榎神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|川島松倉町|Q131870131}} * 川島天神社（{{jalink|若栗神社|Q11616546}}境内社）：愛知県{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}島村 * {{jalink|上ノ島神明神社|Q11358370}}：岐阜県各務原市川島松倉町 * {{jalink|社宮神社 (各務原市)||社宮神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|成清町|Q131983825}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市松本町)|Q134987078|白鬚神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|松本町 (各務原市)|Q131870034|松本町}} * {{jalink|八剣神社 (岐南町)|Q11390592|八剣神社}}：岐阜県{{jalink|羽島郡|Q615068}}{{jalink|岐南町|Q1204285}}みやまち 『{{jalink|尾張国風土記|Q135598023}}』逸文によれば、式内社の「川嶋神社」は葉栗郡河沼郷川嶋村に所在し、{{jalink|聖武天皇|Q349263}}の時代に{{jalink|白鹿|Q72616124}}が時々現れたので勅命で建てられた神社という。 === 祭神 === * 主神 ** {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} * 合祀 ** {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐男尊}} ** {{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主神}} ** {{jalink|徳川義直|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|徳川光友|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|川島神社本殿]] === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「本郷口」バス停下車、徒歩15分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}} {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷」行き ** 名古屋鉄道{{jalink|名鉄犬山線|Q6810411|犬山線}} {{jalink|江南駅 (愛知県)|Q201473|江南駅}}より「川島」行き === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {'text': '愛知県江南市宮田町四ツ谷285', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:川島神社 (江南市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11478285}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 |名称 = 川島神社 |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|川島神社鳥居]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}宮田町四ツ谷285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社
{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|牛頭天王|Q11570247}} |例祭 = 3月26日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=川島神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''川島神社'''（かわしまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|江南市|Q820496}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「川嶋神社」の論社の一つであるが、異説も多い。 === 歴史 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''牛頭天王'''と称しており、度重なる洪水で廃れたという。所在地はかつて「天神森」と呼ばれていた。江戸中期に『延喜式神名帳』の葉栗郡「川嶋神社」に比定する説が浮上し、再建された。{{jalink|1898年|Q7848}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}31年）には正式に「川島神社」と改称した。 ただし式内社の「川嶋神社」は論社は複数あり学説としては確定していない。これは付近に{{jalink|木曽川|Q779607}}があり、度重なる洪水で社殿の流失があったため正確には不明であるためとされる。論社としては以下のようなものがある。 * 川島神社：愛知県江南市宮田町 * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市川島笠田町)|Q124668655|白鬚神社}}：{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|川島笠田町|Q131938355}} * {{jalink|榎神社 (各務原市)|Q134893091|榎神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|川島松倉町|Q131870131}} * 川島天神社（{{jalink|若栗神社|Q11616546}}境内社）：愛知県{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}島村 * {{jalink|上ノ島神明神社|Q11358370}}：岐阜県各務原市川島松倉町 * {{jalink|社宮神社 (各務原市)||社宮神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|成清町|Q131983825}} * {{jalink|白鬚神社 (各務原市松本町)|Q134987078|白鬚神社}}：岐阜県各務原市{{jalink|松本町 (各務原市)|Q131870034|松本町}} * {{jalink|八剣神社 (岐南町)|Q11390592|八剣神社}}：岐阜県{{jalink|羽島郡|Q615068}}{{jalink|岐南町|Q1204285}}みやまち 『{{jalink|尾張国風土記|Q135598023}}』逸文によれば、式内社の「川嶋神社」は葉栗郡河沼郷川嶋村に所在し、{{jalink|聖武天皇|Q349263}}の時代に{{jalink|白鹿|Q72616124}}が時々現れたので勅命で建てられた神社という。 === 祭神 === * 主神 ** {{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|水波売神}} * 合祀 ** {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐男尊}} ** {{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主神}} ** {{jalink|徳川義直|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|徳川光友|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|川島神社本殿]] === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「本郷口」バス停下車、徒歩15分 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}} {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「宮田本郷」行き ** 名古屋鉄道{{jalink|名鉄犬山線|Q6810411|犬山線}} {{jalink|江南駅 (愛知県)|Q201473|江南駅}}より「川島」行き === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Kōnan|Q820496}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhame-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine
{{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kōnan|Q820496}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}} in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{jalink|1898|Q7848}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{jalink|Kiso River|Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355}}, {{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}} * {{jalink|Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|Enoki Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{jalink|Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, Aichi * {{jalink|Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)||Shamiya Shrine}}: {{jalink|Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{jalink|Ginan|Q1204285}}, {{jalink|Hashima District|Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{jalink|Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{jalink|white deer|Q72616124}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Shōmu|Q349263}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhame-no-Kami}} * Merged deities ** {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susao-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005|Bisai Line}}, and {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{jalink|Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|Kōnan Station}} on Meitetsu {{jalink|Inuyama Line|Q6810411|Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわしましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawashima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawashima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kōnan|Q820496}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}} in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{jalink|1898|Q7848}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{jalink|Kiso River|Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355}}, {{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}} * {{jalink|Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|Enoki Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{jalink|Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, Aichi * {{jalink|Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)||Shamiya Shrine}}: {{jalink|Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{jalink|Ginan|Q1204285}}, {{jalink|Hashima District|Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{jalink|Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{jalink|white deer|Q72616124}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Shōmu|Q349263}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhame-no-Kami}} * Merged deities ** {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susao-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005|Bisai Line}}, and {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{jalink|Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|Kōnan Station}} on Meitetsu {{jalink|Inuyama Line|Q6810411|Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわしましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawashima Fudoki|Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{jalink|white deer|Q72616124}} {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kōnan|Q820496}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}} in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{jalink|1898|Q7848}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{jalink|Kiso River|Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355}}, {{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}} * {{jalink|Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|Enoki Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{jalink|Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, Aichi * {{jalink|Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)||Shamiya Shrine}}: {{jalink|Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{jalink|Ginan|Q1204285}}, {{jalink|Hashima District|Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{jalink|Owari no Kuni {'text': '愛知県江南市宮田町四ツ谷285', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:川島神社 (江南市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11478285}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = June 2024}} {{神社 |名称 = Kawashima Shrine |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Kōnan|Q820496}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhame-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine
{{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kōnan|Q820496}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}} in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{jalink|1898|Q7848}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{jalink|Kiso River|Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355}}, {{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}} * {{jalink|Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|Enoki Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{jalink|Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, Aichi * {{jalink|Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)||Shamiya Shrine}}: {{jalink|Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{jalink|Ginan|Q1204285}}, {{jalink|Hashima District|Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{jalink|Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{jalink|white deer|Q72616124}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Shōmu|Q349263}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhame-no-Kami}} * Merged deities ** {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susao-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005|Bisai Line}}, and {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{jalink|Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|Kōnan Station}} on Meitetsu {{jalink|Inuyama Line|Q6810411|Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわしましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawashima [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Kōnan|Q820496}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhame-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine
{{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kōnan|Q820496}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}} in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{jalink|1898|Q7848}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{jalink|Kiso River|Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355}}, {{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}} * {{jalink|Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|Enoki Shrine}}: {{jalink|Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{jalink|Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, Aichi * {{jalink|Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)||Shamiya Shrine}}: {{jalink|Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|Shirahige Shrine}}: {{jalink|Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{jalink|Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{jalink|Ginan|Q1204285}}, {{jalink|Hashima District|Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{jalink|Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{jalink|white deer|Q72616124}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Shōmu|Q349263}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhame-no-Kami}} * Merged deities ** {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susao-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245}} ** {{jalink|Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005|Bisai Line}}, and {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{jalink|Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|Kōnan Station}} on Meitetsu {{jalink|Inuyama Line|Q6810411|Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわしましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawashima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawashima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|UNKNOWN}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)||WD=|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami}} * Merged deities ** {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susao-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245|WD=Q843245}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line}}, and {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{ill|lt=Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|WD=Q201473|lt=Kōnan Station}} on Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami}} * Merged deities ** {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susao-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245|WD=Q843245}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line}}, and {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{ill|lt=Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|WD=Q201473|lt=Kōnan Station}} on Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} {'text': '愛知県江南市宮田町四ツ谷285', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:川島神社 (江南市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11478285}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed| date = June 2024}} {{神社 |名称 = Kawashima Shrine |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami}} * Merged deities ** {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susao-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245|WD=Q843245}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line}}, and {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{ill|lt=Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|WD=Q201473|lt=Kōnan Station}} on Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami}} * Merged deities ** {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susao-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245|WD=Q843245}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line}}, and {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{ill|lt=Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|WD=Q201473|lt=Kōnan Station}} on Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} content == {{Citation needed| date = June 2024}} {{神社 |名称 = Kawashima Shrine |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848|1=1898}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546|1=Waguri Shrine}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370|1=Kaminoshima-Shinmei Shrine}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825|1=Narukiyo-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō|1=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285|1=Ginan, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023|1=Owari no Kuni Fudoki}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124|1=white deer}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. === Enshrined content == {{Citation needed| date = June 2024}} {{神社 |名称 = Kawashima Shrine |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848|1=1898}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546|1=Waguri Shrine}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370|1=Kaminoshima-Shinmei Shrine}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825|1=Narukiyo-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō|1=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285|1=Ginan, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023|1=Owari no Kuni Fudoki}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124|1=white deer}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} * Merged deities ** {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susao-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186|1=Tokugawa Yoshinao}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245|WD=Q843245|1=Tokugawa Mitsutomo}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Transportation === * 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Hongōguchi" stop ** from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, and {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{ill|lt=Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|WD=Q201473|lt=Kōnan Station|1=Kōnan Station (Aichi)}} on Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, bound for "Kawashima" === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわしましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawashima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawashima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] content == {{Citation needed| date = June 2024}} {{神社 |名称 = Kawashima Shrine |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848|1=1898}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546|1=Waguri Shrine}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370|1=Kaminoshima-Shinmei Shrine}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825|1=Narukiyo-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō|1=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285|1=Ginan, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023|1=Owari no Kuni Fudoki}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124|1=white deer}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. === Enshrined Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848|1=1898}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Shrine}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370|1=Kaminoshima-Shinmei Shrine}}: Kawashima (Ginan)}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285|1=Ginan, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023|1=Owari no Kuni Fudoki}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by content == {{Citation needed| date = June 2024}} {{神社 |名称 = Kawashima Shrine |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848|1=1898}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546|1=Waguri Shrine}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370|1=Kaminoshima-Shinmei Shrine}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825|1=Narukiyo-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō|1=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285|1=Ginan, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023|1=Owari no Kuni Fudoki}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124|1=white deer}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} * Merged deities ** {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susao-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186|1=Tokugawa Yoshinao}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Q843245|WD=Q843245|1=Tokugawa Mitsutomo}} ** {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} [[File:川島神社本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden of Kawashima Shrine]] === Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, and {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Bus Terminal stop 3), bound for "Miyata Hongō" ** from {{ill|lt=Kōnan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|WD=Q201473|lt=Kōnan Station|1=Kōnan Station (Aichi)}} on Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, content == {{Citation needed| date = June 2024}} {{神社 |名称 = Kawashima Shrine |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848|1=1898}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546|1=Waguri Shrine}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370|1=Kaminoshima-Shinmei Shrine}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825|1=Narukiyo-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|WD=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō|1=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285|1=Ginan, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|WD=Q135598023|1=Owari no Kuni Fudoki}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124|1=white deer}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} * Merged deities ** {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susao-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
