content == {{Citation needed| date = June 2024}} {{神社 |名称 = Kawashima Shrine |画像 = [[File:川島神社.JPG|250px|Torii of Kawashima Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|WD=Q7848|1=1898}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|WD=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|WD=Q11616546|1=Waguri Shrine}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|WD=Q11358370|1=Kaminoshima-Shinmei Shrine}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|WD=Q131983825|1=Narukiyo-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|WD=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō|1=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|WD=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|WD=Q1204285|1=Ginan, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|WD=Q72616124|1=white deer}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity ** {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} * Merged deities ** {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susao-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|qid=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}Miyata-chō Yotsuya 285 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|qid=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhame-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} |例祭 = March 26 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kawashima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawashima Shrine''' (かわしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan|Q820496|qid=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Kawashima Shrine" in Haguri District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', though there are many differing theories. === History === Its founding time is unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}}, it was called '''Gozu Tennō''', and is said to have declined due to repeated floods. The site was formerly called "Tenjinmori." In the mid-Edo period, a theory emerged identifying it as the Haguri District "Kawashima Shrine" in the Engishiki register, and it was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1898|Q7848|qid=Q7848|1=1898}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 31), it was officially renamed "Kawashima Shrine." However, the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" has multiple candidate shrines and is not settled in scholarship. This is said to be because the nearby {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} repeatedly flooded and washed away shrine buildings, making exact identification unclear. Candidate shrines include: * Kawashima Shrine: Miyata-chō, Konan, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Kasada-chō Kawashima, Kakamigahara)|Q124668655|qid=Q124668655|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|qid=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|qid=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|qid=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine (precinct shrine of {{ill|lt=Wakaguri Shrine|Q11616546|qid=Q11616546|1=Waguri Shrine}}): Shimamura, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, Aichi * {{ill|lt=Uenoshima Shinmei Shrine|Q11358370|qid=Q11358370|1=Kaminoshima-Shinmei Shrine}}: Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shamiya Shrine (Kakamigahara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shamiya Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Narukiyo-chō|Q131983825|qid=Q131983825|1=Narukiyo-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine (Matsumoto-chō, Kakamigahara)|Q134987078|qid=Q134987078|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine}}: {{ill|lt=Matsumoto-chō (Kakamigahara)|Q131870034|qid=Q131870034|lt=Matsumoto-chō|1=Matsumoto-chō}}, Kakamigahara, Gifu * {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)|Q11390592|qid=Q11390592|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (Ginan)}}: Miyamachi, {{ill|lt=Ginan|Q1204285|qid=Q1204285|1=Ginan, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|qid=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}, Gifu According to surviving passages of the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Fudoki|Q135598023|qid=Q135598023|1=Owari no Kuni Fudoki}}', the Shikinaisha "Kawashima Shrine" was in Kawashima Village, Kawanuma-gō, Haguri District, and was a shrine built by imperial order after a {{ill|lt=white deer|Q72616124|qid=Q72616124|1=white deer}} appeared from time to time in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|qid=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. === Enshrined
